title: Salty Fermented Argentinian Wheat Muffins
description: A nostalgic Argentinian breakfast featuring aerated, salty wheat muffins with a gentle fermentation for a light, airy texture.
instructions: In a bowl mix the wheat flour, salt, yeast, baking powder, and cornstarch.
In another bowl whisk together warm water, warm milk, olive oil, honey, and the beaten egg.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry mixture and stir until a sticky dough forms.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the dough ferment at room temperature for 1 hour, until doubled in size.
Preheat the oven to 190°C and line a muffin tin with paper liners.
Divide the dough into 6 equal portions and place each into a muffin cup.
Bake for 15 minutes, or until the tops are golden and a toothpick comes out clean.
Remove from the oven and let the muffins cool in the tin for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.
Serve warm with a side of chimichurri or a sprinkle of smoked paprika for a nostalgic Argentinian touch.
